title: Vi Vision Decision Option
description:
One CHOICE within a decision (decision.schema.json options[]).

Design intent:
- An option is HUMAN-first: label is the choice in Tim's terms; implication says what choosing it entails, so the decision can be made on MEANING, not machine values.
- recommended marks the agent/RHM's suggested choice (the operator still decides; a recommendation is not a resolve).
- Options are DATA the decision-card archetype renders; the take records WHICH option's value was chosen (as a decision_take mark on the decision's address).

Relationships:
- Embedded in decision.options[].
- The chosen option becomes the decision's decided_value (resolved from the take mark, registry-is-truth).

label.description: The choice in HUMAN terms (Tim's words) — what picking this means. The decided_value the take records.

description.description: Optional fuller explanation of the choice. RENDERS AS PROSE (a readable body line) — NOT inside the mono chip (against the bar 2026-06-19: description jammed into the implication chip is the weakest pixel on every card).

implication.description: What choosing this option entails / its consequence — so the decision is made on meaning, not a bare label. RENDERS AS THE TERSE MONO CHIP (implication only; keep it telegraphic — a fragment, not a sentence). The chip is reserved for this; description goes to prose.

recommended.description: Whether this is the recommended choice (the RHM's suggestion; the operator still decides). Default false.

dimensions.description: ★ DATA-BOUND DEVICE signal (re-baseline 2026-06-19): this option's VALUES on the decision's named trade-off axes (decision.dimensions) — the REAL signal a visual device ENCODES (a bar's height, a matrix cell, a duo position). Keyed by the dimension id (must match a key in decision.dimensions). A device renders THESE values, NEVER hardcoded heights — a device with no real dimension values is decoration and MUST NOT ship (Tim: 'a stupid bar chart with no meaning'). Optional: present only when the decision has genuine multi-dimensional trade-offs (a simple yes/no carries none → no device). SUBORDINATE TO LEGIBILITY: the option's label/description/implication stay present + legible regardless of the device — the device never clamps or hides them.

additionalProperties.description: The option's value on this dimension — a 0..1 magnitude the device scales, OR a short human level label. REAL, per-option; never a hardcoded render value.
